steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: On both sides of the blank are independent clauses — “how this works” is a noun clause functioning as the direct object of 'reveals,' and “the hole's small size restricts light...” is a complete clause with its own subject and verb. Neither side is a dependent clause or phrase.

steps.title: Apply the correct punctuation rule
steps.content:
The correct choice uses a period followed by a comma after the conjunctive adverb 'Because' (B).

This separates the two independent clauses with a full stop, then properly introduces the dependent clause “Because the hole's small size…” that explains the reason.

All other options either split sentences incorrectly (A,C with comma splice), use a colon inappropriately (D), or violate the SAT's strict preference for avoiding sentence fragments starting with 'Because' (B is the only one conforming to this).
